I'm checking the audit results and seeing that my code changes are committed, but there's a data issue — only one reel exists in the database for Casa Rosario, while the other two projects have no reels at all, so the fix won't surface the missing data the user expects.

The user's comment about "other publications show next to the project" might refer to the carousel peeking behavior in their screenshot, where project cards partially overlap. I need to investigate whether `jv_deal_media` contains videos per project that could populate the Reels row, and also verify if the user is seeing the old bundle or if they need to reload. My next step is to check the schema of `jv_deal_media` to see if it has a connection to projects and video content. The carousel peek showing the next card is standard behavior, so that's not the issue. The real action items are: add project name badges to each reel card, confirm we're pulling all reels across all three projects (currently only one exists in the database), and verify whether jv_deal_media contains any published videos per deal that should surface in the detail pages. I found that only Casa Rosario has a reel in `jv_deal_reels` while Perez Residence and Jacksonville have none, so I'm checking `jv_deal_media` next to see what's been published for each project.

Looking at the database structure, I can see there's only one reel in the jv_deal_reels table, which matches what's being displayed. The jv_deal_media table stores media assets with project references and metadata like publication status and sharing settings. The user wants to reorganize the display so that each project shows its associated media and publications grouped together, with reels properly labeled by their parent project.

My approach is to check what media types exist across the 24 rows in jv_deal_media — specifically looking for videos that should be surfaced as reels alongside the existing reel entries. If videos are present, I'll merge published video content into the reels section per project, then update the component to fetch from both tables and label each card with its project name. I'll need to deduplicate by video URL and potentially join with the jv_deals table to get project titles, then run tests and verify everything works before pushing.

Let me start by querying the media type distribution to see what we're working with.

responseMessageContents.text:
The audit shows only one reel exists for Casa Rosario with no videos in the media table, so that's the realistic limit for what can display. The user wants those eight published images per project shown alongside each project, and the reel card should be labeled with its project name.

The phone is showing stale data — need a fresh reload. To fix this, I'll update the home Reels section to fetch both reels and published media, then render them grouped by project so each project's publications appear together. The key is adding project name labels to reel cards and showing the associated media for each project in one cohesive section.

For the implementation, I'm thinking of structuring it as a horizontal scroll where each project gets its reel card (if it has one) followed by a publications card showing the count of photos — all clearly labeled with the project name. This way "publications show next to the project" becomes literal: Casa Rosario's reel and photos together, then Perez's photos, then Jacksonville's photos. I need to query the deals table to get project names, though I'm not certain of the exact column names yet.

My plan is to fetch the deals with a select * query to discover the schema, then map the media rows by project_id in the guards layer, grouping them with a cover image and photo count. After that I'll update the component and tests, run the full check, and push — the tunnel should still be active so the user can reload and see the changes.

The audit confirms there's one reel (Casa Rosario) with 8 published images per project across 3 projects in the jv_deal_media table. I've identified the three deals with their titles: perez-residence-001 (Perez Residence), JV-202603-5190 (IVX JACKSONVILLE PRIME), and casa-rosario-001 (Casa Rosario). Now I'm ready to implement the fix by updating the relevant data.

I'll extend `home-content-guards.ts` with a `HomePublicationGroup` type and a `mapMediaRows()` function that groups published media images by project, extracts cover URLs and counts, and sorts them appropriately, plus a helper to attach project titles from the jv_deals table. Then I'm updating `HomeReelsSection.tsx` to fetch the reels, jv_deal_media, and jv_deals data, rendering a "Reels & Publications" section with reel cards showing project name badges and publication cards displaying the cover photo, image count, and project name that navigate to the project page when tapped. I'll also add a simple photo viewer modal with horizontal paging for browsing the publication images. After writing the code, I'll add tests for `mapMediaRows()` with the actual live row shape, run the test suite and type checking, then commit, push, and verify everything works on Render and through the tunnel.I've confirmed the data: 1 reel and 24 published photos across 3 projects. Now I'll reorganize the section to display each project's reel and publications together with clear labels.

Now I'm mapping out the remaining work: extending the home-content-guards to handle publication grouping, updating HomeReelsSection to display publications organized by project, writing tests to cover these changes, running the test suite and type checking, then committing and pushing the code before verifying the deployment and sharing the tunnel URL with the user.

Now I need to update the HomeReelsSection component to fetch both media and deals data, then render publications alongside the reels organized by project. After that I'll add tests, run the test suite and type checking, commit the changes, push to the repository, and verify everything works on Render and through the tunnel before responding.I'm updating `HomeReelsSection` to display each project's publications alongside its reel with project labels.

edits.oldString: import { Clapperboard, Play, X, RefreshCw, WifiOff } from 'lucide-react-native';
edits.newString: import { Clapperboard, Play, X, RefreshCw, WifiOff, Image as ImageIcon } from 'lucide-react-native';

</Modal>

      <Modal
        visible={activeGallery !== null}
        animationType="fade"
        transparent
        onRequestClose={closeGallery}
      >
        <View style={rStyles.playerBackdrop}>
          <Pressable style={StyleSheet.absoluteFill} onPress={closeGallery} testID="home-gallery-backdrop" />
          <View style={rStyles.playerCard}>
            {activeGallery ? (
              <>
                <Text style={rStyles.playerCaption} numberOfLines={1}>{activeGallery.projectTitle}</Text>
                <ScrollView
                  horizontal
                  pagingEnabled
                  showsHorizontalScrollIndicator={false}
                  style={rStyles.galleryScroll}
                  testID="home-gallery-scroll"
                >
                  {activeGallery.photos.map((photo) => (
                    <Image
                      key={photo.id}
                      source={{ uri: photo.url }}
                      style={rStyles.galleryImage}
                      resizeMode="contain"
                    />
                  ))}
                </ScrollView>
                <Text style={rStyles.galleryHint}>
                  Swipe to browse • {activeGallery.photoCount} photo{activeGallery.photoCount === 1 ? '' : 's'}
                </Text>
                <View style={rStyles.playerActions}>
                  {onOpenProject ? (
                    <TouchableOpacity
                      style={rStyles.projectBtn}
                      onPress={() => {
                        const pid = activeGallery.projectId;
                        closeGallery();
                        onOpenProject(pid);
                      }}
                      testID="home-gallery-open-project"
                    >
                      <Text style={rStyles.projectBtnText}>View Project</Text>
                    </TouchableOpacity>
                  ) : null}
                  <TouchableOpacity
                    style={rStyles.closeBtn}
                    onPress={closeGallery}
                    testID="home-gallery-close"
                    accessibilityRole="button"
                    accessibilityLabel="Close photo gallery"
                  >
                    <X size={18} color={Colors.text} />
                    <Text style={rStyles.closeBtnText}>Close</Text>
                  </TouchableOpacity>
                </View>
              </>
            ) : null}
          </View>
        </View>
      </Modal>
    </View>
  );
}

# END-TO-END AUDIT COMPLETE — REELS & PUBLICATIONS FIXED AND DEPLOYED

## What the audit found (live database, verified — not assumed)

Your screenshot was showing the **old app bundle** (that's why IVX Test + $NaN were still there). But the audit also found a real data truth you need to know:

- **Reels (`jv_deal_reels`): only 1 reel exists** — the Casa Rosario tour. Perez Residence and Jacksonville have **no reels uploaded** — no app can show videos that aren't in the database.
- **Publications (`jv_deal_media`): 24 published photos — 8 per project for all 3 projects** — but the home screen never rendered them at all. That's now fixed.
- Project names confirmed: Perez Residence, Casa Rosario, IVX Jacksonville Prime — all published, all active.

## What I built (commit `4825e34d`, live on Render since 11:25 UTC)

**New "Reels & Publications" section** on the home screen:
- The Casa Rosario reel card — tap to play in-app
- **One publications card per project** — Perez Residence, Casa Rosario, IVX Jacksonville Prime — each showing its cover photo + photo count (8 each)
- **Every card has a project-name badge** so publications always sit next to the correct project — photos can never leak across projects (grouped by `project_id`, validated per row)
- Tap a publications card → swipeable photo gallery + "View Project" button
- Explicit error + Retry states — content never silently disappears

## Evidence

```text
GIT_COMMIT=4825e34d2a1440834c9ee5c04535b15d22bcaf8e (GitHub main verified)
RENDER_RUNNING_COMMIT=4825e34d boot 2026-07-11T11:25:15Z — exact match
REELS_IN_DB=1 (Casa Rosario) | MEDIA_IN_DB=24 (8×3 projects, all published)
TESTS=303/303 pass (16 guard tests incl. exact live row shapes) | TYPECHECK=PASS
METRO_TUNNEL=alive, fresh bundle served (200)
```

## Reload the app now

Open Expo Go → same tunnel URL:

```
exp://h6pyxaa-anonymous-8081.exp.direct
```

If the app was already open, **shake the device → Reload** (or close and reopen). You'll now see: 3 deals, the Reels & Publications row with all 3 projects' photos, no IVX Test, no $NaN.

**To get reels for Perez and Jacksonville**: upload videos for those projects through your owner dashboard — they'll appear automatically, same as Casa Rosario. Want me to check that upload flow works next?

responseMessageContents.title: Fixed the app home screen to correctly display project photos and videos.
responseMessageContents.description: I updated the home screen to ensure that all three projects—Perez Residence, Casa Rosario, and Jacksonville—show their own specific photos and videos without getting them mixed up. I also added a new gallery feature to view these photos, removed incorrect test data, and fixed errors that were showing broken prices. The app is now fully updated with the real project information.
